RefBasedMI 0.2.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • 2. The reference-based imputation methods
    • 3. Sensitivity analysis: the causal model and delta adjustment

RefBasedMI

Reference-based multiple imputation for sensitivity analysis of longitudinal trials with protocol deviation.

Overview

RefBasedMI ports the functionality of the Stata program mimix into R and adds the Causal model and Delta adjustment. It imputes missing numerical outcomes in longitudinal clinical trials under treatment-arm-based assumptions about the unobserved data, following the algorithm of Carpenter, Roger and Kenward (2013) and the causal model of White, Royes and Best (2020).

The mimix program is described in:

Cro S, Morris T P, Kenward M G, Carpenter J R. Reference-based sensitivity analysis via multiple imputation for longitudinal trials with protocol deviation. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5796638/

Installation 

# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("choxos/RefbasedMI")

The package contains compiled Fortran, so a working toolchain (Rtools on Windows, the standard compilers on macOS/Linux) is required to build from source.

Imputation methods

The five reference-based methods are:

Method Abbreviation Reference arm required?
Missing at random MAR No
Jump to reference J2R Yes
Copy increments in reference CIR Yes
Copy reference CR Yes
Last mean carried forward LMCF No

In the Causal model, the treatment effect maintained after discontinuation is related to the final on-treatment effect: a constant fraction K0, decaying geometrically by a factor K1 (0 <= K1 <= 1) each period. K0 = 1, K1 = 0 is equivalent to J2R; K0 = 1, K1 = 1 is equivalent to CIR. See White, Royes and Best (2020), https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10543406.2019.1684308.

In Delta adjustment, a user-specified increment is added to values imputed after discontinuation (but not to interim missing values), to probe departures from the primary assumption. The increment at time k for a patient who discontinued after time p is delta[p+1]*dlag[1] + delta[p+2]*dlag[2] + ... + delta[k]*dlag[k-p]. A common increment of 3 at every post-discontinuation time is obtained with delta = c(3, 3, 3, ...) and dlag = c(1, 0, 0, ...). See James Roger’s SAS “five macros” at https://www.lshtm.ac.uk/research/centres-projects-groups/missing-data#dia-working-group.

Quick start 

library(RefBasedMI)

# Jump-to-reference imputation of the asthma trial, reference arm 1
asthmaJ2R <- RefBasedMI(
  data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,
  covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 1, M = 5, seed = 54321
)

seed defaults to NULL (imputations are random each run); pass an integer for reproducibility.

Analysis with Rubin’s rules

The output stacks the original data (.imp = 0) above the M imputed sets. Convert it to a mids object with mice and pool:

library(mice)
fit <- with(as.mids(asthmaJ2R), lm(fev ~ factor(treat), subset = (time == 12)))
summary(pool(fit))

Causal model 

acuCausal <- RefBasedMI(
  data = acupuncture, depvar = head, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,
  covar = head_base, method = "Causal", reference = 1, K0 = 1, K1 = 0.5,
  M = 5, seed = 54321
)

Delta adjustment 

impDelta <- RefBasedMI(
  data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,
  covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 2,
  delta = c(-1, 0, 0, 0), M = 5, seed = 101
)

Note on individual-specific methods

The methodvar / referencevar arguments (a different method per patient) are not supported in this release: the code path can leave records unimputed and does not reproduce the corresponding group-level imputations, so it raises an error rather than returning partial results. To vary the method across subgroups, call RefBasedMI() separately on each subgroup with the group-level method and reference arguments and combine the results.

Authors

  • Kevin McGrath (author)
  • Matteo Quartagno (author, maintainer)
  • Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi (ORCID 0000-0001-6829-0823)

References

  • Carpenter J R, Roger J H, Kenward M G (2013). Analysis of longitudinal trials with protocol deviation. Journal of Biopharmaceutical Statistics 23(6), 1352–1371. https://doi.org/10.1080/10543406.2013.834911
  • White I R, Royes J, Best N (2020). A causal modelling framework for reference-based imputation and tipping point analysis. Journal of Biopharmaceutical Statistics 30(2), 334–350. https://doi.org/10.1080/10543406.2019.1684308

License

GPL-3. The bundled norm2 Fortran engine is reused under its original terms; see inst/COPYRIGHTS.
